meaning: n.鞋底钉,铁钩,攀登用鞋底钉
examples: During the descent, the rope got stuck in Pasangs crampon and was torn away from his pack and fell down the abyss.
Lifting the foot onto vague terrain and using the points differently allow one to pull with crampon rather than simply push.
The next day, they rested, but the following day another try was made, taking a different route. Passang had only one crampon. After major difficulties, they headed back again.
Nine crampon-footed hikers crunch up the slope of Athabasca Glacier before stopping to catch their vaporous breath.
Sir Edmund Hillary's ghost would come down and beat you senseless with a crampon, but you could wear it.
